vocabulary.title: NASA APOD API Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Controlled vocabulary and term definitions for the NASA Astronomy Picture of the Day API

terms.label: Astronomy Picture of the Day
terms.definition: A daily feature on the NASA website presenting a new astronomical image or photograph, along with a brief explanation written by a professional astronomer. Published since June 16, 1995.

terms.label: APOD Date
terms.definition: A calendar date in YYYY-MM-DD format referencing a specific APOD entry. Valid dates range from 1995-06-16 (the first APOD publication date) to the current date. Future dates are not available.

terms.label: Media Type
terms.definition: Indicates whether the APOD content for a given date is an image or a video. Possible values are 'image' and 'video'.

terms.label: High-Definition URL
terms.definition: The URL pointing to the high-resolution version of the APOD image. Only present for image entries and only when a high-resolution version exists at the APOD source. The 'hd' query parameter is ignored; HD URLs are always returned when available.

terms.label: Image or Video URL
terms.definition: The standard-resolution URL for the APOD image, or the URL for the APOD video (e.g., a YouTube embed URL) for video entries.

terms.label: Explanation
terms.definition: A written explanation of the APOD image or video authored by a professional astronomer. Describes the astronomical subject, its significance, and relevant scientific context.

terms.label: Title
terms.definition: The title given to the Astronomy Picture of the Day entry by its author.

terms.label: Copyright
terms.definition: The name of the copyright holder for the APOD image or video. Absent when the content is NASA-owned or otherwise in the public domain. When present, re-displaying the imagery requires attribution.

terms.label: Concept Tags
terms.definition: Boolean flag controlling whether keyword concepts are extracted and returned from the APOD explanation text. Concepts are derived from common search tags associated with the description, useful for auto-generated hashtags or content discovery. Note: This feature may be disabled in the current service deployment.

terms.label: Concepts
terms.definition: An ordered dictionary mapping integer index strings to concept keywords derived from the APOD explanation. Only returned when concept_tags=True. Keys are string representations of integers (e.g., '0', '1', '2').

terms.label: Thumbnails
terms.definition: Boolean flag controlling whether a thumbnail image URL is returned for video APOD entries. When True and the entry is a video, thumbnail_url is included in the response. Ignored for image entries.

terms.label: Thumbnail URL
terms.definition: The URL of a thumbnail image for a video APOD entry. Only included in the response when thumbs=True and media_type='video'.

terms.label: Random Count
terms.definition: A positive integer (1-100) specifying the number of randomly selected APOD entries to return. When used, the response is a JSON array of randomly chosen entries. Mutually exclusive with date, start_date, and end_date parameters.

terms.label: Start Date
terms.definition: A date string in YYYY-MM-DD format marking the beginning of a date range query. All APOD entries from start_date through end_date (or today if end_date is omitted) are returned as a JSON array. Cannot be combined with date or count.

terms.label: End Date
terms.definition: A date string in YYYY-MM-DD format marking the end of a date range query. Used with start_date. Defaults to the current date if start_date is specified without end_date. Cannot be combined with date or count.

terms.label: API Key
terms.definition: A NASA API key used to authenticate requests and track usage. DEMO_KEY is available for testing with reduced rate limits (30 requests/hour, 50 requests/day per IP). Register at https://api.nasa.gov/#signUp for a full key.

terms.label: Service Version
terms.definition: The version of the APOD API service that fulfilled the request. Currently 'v1'. Included in every response.

terms.label: High-Definition Flag (Legacy)
terms.definition: A legacy boolean parameter originally intended to control whether high-resolution image URLs are returned. This parameter is now always ignored by the service; HD URLs are returned whenever they exist regardless of this flag.
